groups.theme: Sleep content quality and Sleepcast experience
example_reviews: The Sleepcasts are absolutely incredible. I fall asleep to The Garden every night and I am out within 10 minutes.
The Ocean Sleepcast is my absolute favourite. I can visualise the scene they describe and my brain just switches off.
I downloaded Headspace specifically for the Sleepcasts and they do not disappoint.

groups.theme: Guided meditation, courses, and Andy Puddicombe experience
example_reviews: Andy Puddicombe is a gifted teacher. His voice and pacing in the guided meditations make even complex mindfulness concepts feel accessible.
The 10-day Basics course completely changed my understanding of meditation.
The SOS meditation is a lifesaver during panic attacks. Having a guided emergency meditation available in three taps has helped me manage my anxiety disorder.

groups.theme: Subscription cost, paywalls, and billing issues
example_reviews: 12.99 a month is too expensive for what is essentially an audio app.
The annual subscription at 69.99 works out to under 6 dollars a month which is reasonable for the quality and breadth of content.
Getting a refund is impossible. Their customer service told me to contact Google Play and Google Play told me to contact Headspace.

groups.theme: Technical bugs, crashes, and background audio failures
example_reviews: The app crashes every time I try to download sessions for offline use on my Pixel 8.
Background audio stops playing the moment I switch to another app on Android 14. Complete deal breaker.
My meditation streak reset to zero after a server outage that was not my fault. I had a 92-day streak.

groups.theme: Content variety, personalization, and feature breadth
example_reviews: The mood check-in at the start of each session is a great feature but the recommendations it generates do not seem to actually respond to what I tell it.
The outdoor walking meditation series is a rare combination of movement and mindfulness that no other app does as well.
Content for children is excellent. My 8 year old daughter uses the kids section independently.
